skill.name: docs
skill.description: Research official documentation, API references, examples, and library best practices with concise explanations.
skill.summary: Finds documentation and explains APIs with source-focused guidance.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: The static high-severity finding for a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. Line 3 is only a natural-language description about researching documentation, APIs, examples, and libraries; no cryptographic algorithm, executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or unsafe tool use was found.

content.value_statement: Developers need fast answers from reliable documentation without losing context. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find references, explain APIs, and summarize version caveats.
actual_capabilities: Prioritizes official documentation and canonical examples when researching technical questions.
Explains library APIs with concise language and concrete usage context.
Finds examples that help compare common implementation patterns.
Highlights known version-specific details, limitations, and caveats.
Summarizes key documentation points before providing supporting references.
limitations: It does not edit files or write code directly because write and edit tools are disabled.
It depends on available documentation quality and may need a library version for precise answers.
It does not execute examples or verify runtime behavior in a project environment.
It should not replace legal, security, or production review for critical decisions.

use_cases.title: Understand a New API
use_cases.description: Get a clear explanation of a method, option, or configuration field from official references.

use_cases.title: Compare Library Patterns
use_cases.description: Find documented examples that show recommended setup, migration, or integration approaches.

use_cases.title: Prepare Documentation Notes
use_cases.description: Summarize important behavior, caveats, and source links for a team handoff or review.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an API
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner question about one documented feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the official documentation for [API or feature]. Start with the main purpose, then list key options and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Usage Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Find official or canonical examples for [library task]. Summarize the recommended approach and note version requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Approaches
prompt_templates.scenario: Team is choosing between documented patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the documented approaches for [option A] and [option B]. Explain tradeoffs, caveats, and when each is appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Claims
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of accuracy and source support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these claims about [library or API] against official documentation. Identify unsupported claims, outdated guidance, and missing caveats.

output_examples.input: Explain when to use a cleanup function in a UI effect hook.
output: A cleanup function is used when a side effect creates something that must be removed later.
Common cases include subscriptions, timers, event listeners, and in-flight work that should be canceled.
The answer should mention version-specific behavior when the framework documentation has changed.

output_examples.input: Find the recommended way to configure retries in a client library.
output: The response starts with the official configuration name and where it is documented.
It summarizes the default behavior, supported options, and known limits.
It calls out when retry behavior differs by transport, version, or runtime environment.

output_examples.input: Check whether our migration note matches the current docs.
output: The response separates confirmed claims from claims that need correction.
It flags missing prerequisites and deprecated terms.
It suggests concise replacement wording based on the documented behavior.

best_practices: Provide the library name, version, runtime, and framework when asking for documentation research.
Ask for official sources first when accuracy matters for implementation or migration work.
Request caveats and unsupported assumptions when reviewing existing technical guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for production decisions without naming the version or deployment environment.
Do not treat summarized examples as tested code without running them in your project.
Do not rely on unofficial posts when official documentation answers the question clearly.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps research official documentation, explain APIs, find examples, and summarize library guidance.

faq.question: Can it write or edit project files?
faq.answer: No. The skill metadata disables write and edit tools, so it is focused on research and explanation.

faq.question: Does it use official documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Its instructions prefer official documentation and canonical examples when available.

faq.question: Can it handle version-specific questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It is instructed to highlight version-specific details and caveats when known.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for documentation research tasks.

faq.question: What should I include in a prompt?
faq.answer: Include the library name, version, runtime, and the exact behavior or API you need explained.
